name: linkedin-publish

user-invocable: true
metadata: {"openclaw":{"requires":{"env":["LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID","LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET","LINKEDIN_REDIRECT_URI"]}}}
triggers:
  - "publish to linkedin"
  - "post to linkedin"
  - "share on linkedin"
  - "linkedin article"
  - "publish article to linkedin"
  - "post article on linkedin"
  - "linkedin post"
  - "share article linkedin"
  - "cross-post to linkedin"
  - "connect linkedin"
  - "link linkedin account"
  - "linkedin post with image"
  - "post image to linkedin"
  - "share image on linkedin"
---

# LinkedIn Publish Skill

> ⚠️ READ THIS FIRST — MANDATORY RULES:
> 1. **NEVER write scripts to /tmp or anywhere else.** The helper already exists.
> 2. **NEVER use grep/cut/sed/cat/read-tool to get the access token.** The helper handles it.
> 3. **NEVER create a new bash script for LinkedIn.** Use ONLY `{baseDir}/li-post.sh`.
> 4. **NEVER analyze or describe an attached image.** Pass its path directly to li-post.sh.
> 5. **DO NOT narrate steps.** Run the command, show the result.
> 6. **ONE PUBLISH PER REQUEST.** Call `li-post.sh` exactly once per confirmed
>    request. The instant you get an `OK:` line, the post is LIVE and this flow
>    is DONE — report the URL and STOP. Never call `li-post.sh` again to "retry",
>    "improve the image", or "use the real image". Each call creates a brand-new
>    LinkedIn post; LinkedIn has no edit-with-media API. If you couldn't get the
>    image you wanted, do NOT silently post without it — ask the user first
>    (see **Single-publish guard** below).

---

## Single-publish guard (READ BEFORE EVERY PUBLISH)

Publishing is irreversible and creates a permanent post. To avoid the
duplicate-post problem:

- **Resolve the image BEFORE the single `li-post.sh` call.** Do all image
  retrieval/generation first. Only call `li-post.sh` once everything

---
name: image-gen
description: Generate images from a text prompt via Vertex AI Imagen (LiteLLM gateway). Two modes — casual images delivered to Telegram, and LinkedIn-mode images saved to a file ready to attach to a post.
user-invocable: true
triggers:
  - "generate an image"
  - "create an image"
  - "draw me"
  - "make an image"
  - "make a picture"
  - "create a picture"
  - "generate a photo"
  - "image for my post"
  - "image for linkedin"
  - "create an infographic"
  - "make an infographic"
---

# Image Generation Skill

Generates images from a text prompt using Vertex AI Imagen 4.0 via the LiteLLM
gateway (synchronous curl). Always returns a real file on disk.

## Which mode?

| Situation | Mode |
|-----------|------|
| User just wants a picture sent to them in chat | **Casual mode** |
| Image is meant for a LinkedIn post | **LinkedIn mode** |
| User wants a data chart / stats comparison "infographic" | **Chart mode** (Imagen renders text poorly — use a chart) |

> Do NOT use the native `image_generate` tool for the LinkedIn pipeline. It is
> asynchronous and leaves no file you can attach to a post. Use the curl flow
> below — it returns a deterministic local path. (The native tool is fine only
> for a quick "send me a picture" with nothing to publish.)

## Step 1: Clarify or proceed

If the user gave a clear prompt, continue. If vague (e.g. "generate something
cool"), ask once:
> What would you like me to generate? Describe the scene, style, mood, or subject.

**Aspect ratio:** Imagen accepts `1:1, 2:3, 3:2, 3:4, 4:3, 4:5, 5:4, 9:16,
16:9, 21:9` and a few others. For LinkedIn use `16:9`. `1.91:1` is INVALID.

## Step 2: Generate the image (curl → file)

Pick the output path based on mode:
- **Casual mode:** `OUT=/tmp/generated_image.png`
- **LinkedIn mode:** save under the user's images dir so `linkedin-publish` can
  attach it:
  ```bash
  mkdir -p "/data/workspace/social/linkedin/$TELEGRAM_USER_ID/images"
  OUT="/data/workspace/social/linkedin/$TELEGRA
Command still running (session cool-cove, pid 31718). Use process (list/poll/log/write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.

    "kind": "agentTurn",
    "message": "PUBLISH_NOW: Use li-post.sh to publish this post with the image at /data/workspace/social/linkedin/264468965/images/middle-managers-1783494572.png

POST TEXT:
Your middle managers are drowning in AI adoption. And nobody's talking about it.

New HBR research found something uncomfortable. Most companies treat AI rollout as a tech project. IT installs it. C-suite celebrates it. Headcount reduction gets mentioned.

But there's a layer in the middle getting crushed.

Middle managers are caught between:
• Executives demanding AI transformation NOW
• Teams who need training, guidance, and support
• Zero formal playbook on how to actually do this

They're not just managing people anymore. They're managing:
→ Tool selection and procurement
→ Training and upskilling programs
→ Change management across teams
→ Security and compliance concerns
→ Productivity expectations from above

All while doing their actual jobs.

Here's the hard truth most leaders miss:
AI doesn't eliminate middle management — it makes it harder.

The people who used to just execute now have to translate between human needs and AI capabilities. That's a new skill. And nobody taught it to them.

If you're a middle manager feeling this — you're not failing. The playbook just doesn't exist yet.

And if you're a leader? Check in on your middle layer before you lose them.

What's actually helping your managers adapt?

#Leadership #AIAdoption #MiddleManagement #ChangeManagement #FutureOfWork

After publishing, save the post record to /data/workspace/social/

name: linkedin-coach
description: Proactive LinkedIn coach. Checks posting cadence, sends daily nudges, proposes next content idea. Run by cron or manually.
user-invocable: true
metadata: {"openclaw":{"requires":{"env":["LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID"]}}}
triggers:
  - "coach me"
  - "what should i post today"
  - "am i on track"
  - "linkedin check in"
  - "how am i doing on linkedin"
  - "push me to post"
  - "content nudge"
---

# LinkedIn Coach Skill

> ⚠️ MANDATORY RULES:
> 1. **Never publish.** This skill motivates and proposes. Posting uses `linkedin-publish`.
> 2. **Per-user isolation.** All data keyed by `$TELEGRAM_USER_ID` (from env or cron prompt).
> 3. **Always be a coach, not a reporter.** Don't just show stats — interpret them. Push the user to act.
> 4. **DO NOT narrate steps.** Show results directly.

---

## When this skill runs

- **Daily cron** (9 AM user timezone) — called with explicit `TELEGRAM_USER_ID` in prompt
- **Heartbeat session** — owner gets a quick check each session
- **User manually asks** — "what should I post today?", "coach me", etc.

---

## Flow: Daily Coach Check

### Step 1 — Load user context (ONE bash block)

```bash
UID="${TELEGRAM_USER_ID}"
BASE="/data/workspace/social/linkedin/$UID"
TOKEN_STORE=/data/openclaw/linkedin-tokens.json

# OAuth check
ACCESS_TOKEN=*** -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].access_token // empty' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)
DISPLAY_NAME=$(jq -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].display_name // "there"' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)

# Strategy: extract cadence line
CADENCE="unknown"
if [ -f "$BASE/strategy.md" ]; then
  CADENCE=$(grep -i "cadence\|frequency\|per week\|posts/week" "$BASE/strategy.md" | head -1 | sed 's/.*: //')
fi

# Posts in last 7 days
POST_COUNT=$(find "$BASE/posts/" -name "*.md" -newer "$(date -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%d 2>/dev/null || date -v-7d +%Y-%m-%d)" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

# Days since last post
LAST_POST_DATE=$(ls -t "$BASE/posts/"*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{

NAME=Andrii Bidochko
CADENCE=## Posting Cadence
POSTS_LAST_7=0
LAST_POST=2026-07-08
TODAY=2026-07-08
NEXT_IDEA_BLOCK=

# Content Calendar — Andrii Bidochko

---

## Scheduled Posts — Benchmark-First AI Agent Series

### Post 1: The anatomy of a good benchmark (July 7, 9:00 AM UTC)

**Pillar:** Technology & AI innovations
**Key Message:** #4 — Benchmark-first AI Agent development
**Status:** 📅 Scheduled for auto-publish

**Draft:**

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗱𝗶𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗻 𝗮 𝗵𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗮 𝘀𝗲𝗹𝗳-𝗲𝘃𝗼𝗹𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗼𝗻𝗲?

One has a benchmark. The other has vibes.

A good benchmark answers three questions:

1. **What's the task?** (specific, not vague)
2. **What does "done" look like?** (verifiable outcome)
3. **How do you measure it?** (quantifiable metric)

Most teams skip this. They build the agent first, then scramble to figure out if it's working.

Flip it. Write the benchmark BEFORE a single line of code. Make it pass. Then make the agent chase it.

The benchmark IS your reward function. Every iteration moves the needle. Every failure teaches the system.

This is TDD for AI. Test first. Agent second.

What's one AI project you're building without a clear benchmark?

#AI #AIAgents #TDD #MachineLearning #SoftwareEngineering

**Characters:** ~950

---

### Post 2: Why most AI agents drift (July 7, 3:00 PM UTC)

**Pillar:** Technology & AI innovations
**Key Message:** #4 — Benchmark-first AI Agent development
**Status:** ✅ Published — https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:share:7480274563235254272/

**Draft:**

𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗔𝗜 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝗱𝗮𝘆. 𝗜𝘁 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗿𝗼𝘄. 𝗬𝗼𝘂 𝗱𝗼𝗻'𝘁 𝗸𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝘄𝗵𝘆.

Sound familiar?

This is the drift problem. Most agents have no anchor. They optimize for whatever feels right in the moment — but there's no fixed target.

The fix: **benchmark + reward function.**

Define the outcome. Make it measurable. Let the agent optimize toward it.

Every successful iteration is progress. Every failure is a signal. No guessing.

Traditional so

# LinkedIn Daily Brief Skill

> ⚠️ MANDATORY RULES:
> 1. **Draft COMPLETE posts — not angles or ideas.** Every draft must be publish-ready in the user's voice.
> 2. **Approve = immediate publish.** When the user taps ✅ Publish, call `li-post.sh` right away. No extra confirmation.
> 3. **Per-user isolation.** All data keyed by `$TELEGRAM_USER_ID`.
> 4. **Source everything.** Every draft must have a real, recent (≤48h) source URL.
> 5. **DO NOT narrate steps.** Show results directly.
> 6. **Max 3 drafts per day.** Quality over quantity.

---

## When this runs

- **Daily cron** (8 AM user timezone) — fires before the coach check at 9 AM
- **User manually asks** — "give me posts for today", "morning brief", etc.

---

## Flow A: Generate Morning Brief (cron or manual trigger)

### Step 1 — Load user context (ONE bash block)

```bash
UID="${TELEGRAM_USER_ID}"
BASE="/data/workspace/social/linkedin/$UID"
TOKEN_STORE=/data/openclaw/linkedin-tokens.json
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
PENDING_DIR="$BASE/pending"

# OAuth check
ACCESS_TOKEN=*** -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].access_token // empty' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)
DISPLAY_NAME=$(jq -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].display_name // "there"' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)

# Check if brief already sent today
BRIEF_FLAG="$PENDING_DIR/.brief-sent-$TODAY"
[ -f "$BRIEF_FLAG" ] && echo "ALREADY_SENT=true" || echo "ALREADY_SENT=false"

# Load brand voice pillars and tone
PILLARS=""
TONE=""
if [ -f "$BASE/profile.md" ]; then
  PILLARS=$(grep -A 10 "Content Pi

ALREADY_SENT=false

# perplexity-research — Brain-Augmented Web Research

> **Convention:** see [conventions/quality.md](../conventions/quality.md) for
> citation rules; every claim from web research lands with a verifiable
> citation, not a paraphrase.
>
> **Convention:** see [conventions/brain-first.md](../conventions/brain-first.md)
> for the lookup chain. This skill ENFORCES brain-first by sending brain
> context as part of the Perplexity prompt — the web search focuses on
> the delta between brain knowledge and current web state.

## What this does

Combines existing brain knowledge with Perplexity's web search. The
agent sends brain context about a topic into a Perplexity query;
Perplexity searches + reads + synthesizes multiple pages with citations,
focused on what's NEW relative to the supplied context.

**The key insight:** Perplexity doesn't just search — it reads and
synthesizes with citations. By sending brain context in the
instructions, it knows what you already know, so it surfaces the delta
instead of repeating settled fact.

## When to use this vs other tools

| Need | Use |
|------|-----|
| Deep research with citations | **This skill** — Perplexity + Opus |
| Quick URL content | `web_fetch` |
| Brain-only lookup | `gbrain query` / `gbrain search` |
| Real-time social monitoring | external X / social-media collectors |
| Structur

## Scheduled Posts — Benchmark-First AI Agent Series

### Post 1: The anatomy of a good benchmark (July 7, 9:00 AM UTC)

**Pillar:** Technology & AI innovations
**Key Message:** #4 — Benchmark-first AI Agent development
**Status:** 📅 Scheduled for auto-publish

**Draft:**

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗱𝗶𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗻 𝗮 𝗵𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗮 𝘀𝗲𝗹𝗳-𝗲𝘃𝗼𝗹𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗼𝗻𝗲?

One has a benchmark. The other has vibes.

A good benchmark answers three questions:

1. **What's the task?** (specific, not vague)
2. **What does "done" look like?** (verifiable outcome)
3. **How do you measure it?** (quantifiable metric)

Most teams skip this. They build the agent first, then scramble to figure out if it's working.

Flip it. Write the benchmark BEFORE a single line of code. Make it pass. Then make the agent chase it.

The benchmark IS your reward function. Every iteration moves the needle. Every failure teaches the system.

This is TDD for AI. Test first. Agent second.

What's one AI project you're building without a clear benchmark?

#AI #AIAgents #TDD #MachineLearning #SoftwareEngineering

**Characters:** ~950

### Post 2: Why most AI agents drift (July 7, 3:00 PM UTC)

**Pillar:** Technology & AI innovations
**Key Message:** #4 — Benchmark-first AI Agent development
**Status:** ✅ Published — https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:share:7480274563235254272/

**Draft:**

𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗔𝗜 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝗱𝗮𝘆. 𝗜𝘁 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗿𝗼𝘄. 𝗬𝗼𝘂 𝗱𝗼𝗻'𝘁 𝗸𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝘄𝗵𝘆.

Sound familiar?

This is the drift problem. Most agents have no anchor. They optimize for whatever feels right in the moment — but there's no fixed target.

The fix: **benchmark + reward function.**

Define the outcome. Make it measurable. Let the agent optimize toward it.

Every successful iteration is progress. Every failure is a signal. No guessing.

Traditional so

source_date: 2026-06-29

𝗜𝗻𝗳𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝗯𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝘁𝗼 𝗰𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗽𝘀𝗲.

DeepSeek just open-sourced DSpark — a framework that speeds up LLM inference by 85%.

Here's why this matters for you.

Most teams built their AI stack around one provider's pricing. OpenAI. Anthropic. Google. You locked in. Now you're paying their margins.

But inference costs aren't fixed. They're collapsing. Every month, open-source gets faster, cheaper, more competitive.

The gap between "I need GPT-4" and "I can run this locally" is shrinking fast.

If your AI strategy depends on one provider staying expensive — you're betting against the market.

The winners aren't the ones with the biggest AI budget. They're the ones who can swap models without rewriting their stack.

That's the real moat: flexibility, not spending power.

When did you last price-check your inference costs?

#AI #LLM #Inference #OpenSource #AIAgents

date: 2026-07-09
n: 2
pillar: Technology & AI innovations
source: https://venturebeat.com/orchestration/trunk-tools-cut-document-review-from-60-days-to-10-by-ditching-general-purpose-models
source_date: 2026-07-03
status: pending
---

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗔𝗜 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝗶𝘀𝗻'𝘁 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗹. 𝗜𝘁'𝘀 𝘇𝗲𝗿𝗼 𝗯𝗹𝗼𝗮𝘁.

Trunk Tools just cut document review from 60 days to 10.

Their secret? They stopped throwing GPT-4 at everything.

Here's the mistake most teams make:

You pick a model. Then you try to make it do EVERYTHING. Summarization. Extraction. Classification. Generation. One tool, all jobs.

That's not a strategy. That's a crutch.

The winners are doing the opposite. They're building specialized stacks:
→ Small models for simple tasks (fast, cheap)
→ Domain-specific fine-tunes for niche work
→ General-purpose models ONLY when nothing else fits

The result? 6x faster. 80% less spend. Better accuracy.

You don't need a bigger model. You need the RIGHT model for each job.

What's one task you're overpaying to run?

#AI #EnterpriseAI #LLM #AIArchitecture #Efficiency

---
date: 2026-07-09
n: 3
pillar: Technology & AI innovations
source: https://venturebeat.com/orchestration/morgan-stanley-cut-its-riskiest-reconciliation-job-in-half-by-making-its-agents-less-autonomous
source_date: 2026-06-30
status: pending
---

𝗠𝗼𝗿𝗴𝗮𝗻 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗹𝗲𝘆'𝘀 𝗯𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗔𝗜 𝘄𝗶𝗻? 𝗠𝗮𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝗹𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗻𝗼𝗺𝗼𝘂𝘀.

Everyone's chasing fully autonomous AI agents. Self-driving workflows. Zero human input.

Morgan Stanley went the opposite direction. And it worked.

Their riskiest reconciliation job? Cut in half. Not by giving agents MORE freedom. By giving them constraints.

Here's the insight most teams miss:

Autonomy isn't a feature. It's a risk multiplier.

The more freedom an agent has, the more ways it can fail. The harder it is to debug. The less predictable the output.

Morgan Stanley didn't dumb down their AI. They made it focused. Scoped. Accountable.

The result? Fewer errors. Faster resolution. Actual trust from the humans in the loop.

Sometimes the best AI architecture is knowing when to say "stop, ask a human."

Are your agents autonomous — or just unsupervised?

#AI #AIAgents #EnterpriseAI #FinancialServices #Automation

# LinkedIn Daily Brief Skill

> ⚠️ MANDATORY RULES:
> 1. **Draft COMPLETE posts — not angles or ideas.** Every draft must be publish-ready in the user's voice.
> 2. **Approve = immediate publish.** When the user taps ✅ Publish, call `li-post.sh` right away. No extra confirmation.
> 3. **Per-user isolation.** All data keyed by `$TELEGRAM_USER_ID`.
> 4. **Source everything.** Every draft must have a real, recent (≤48h) source URL.
> 5. **DO NOT narrate steps.** Show results directly.
> 6. **Max 3 drafts per day.** Quality over quantity.

---

## When this runs

- **Daily cron** (8 AM user timezone) — fires before the coach check at 9 AM
- **User manually asks** — "give me posts for today", "morning brief", etc.

---

## Flow A: Generate Morning Brief (cron or manual trigger)

### Step 1 — Load user context (ONE bash block)

```bash
UID="${TELEGRAM_USER_ID}"
BASE="/data/workspace/social/linkedin/$UID"
TOKEN_STORE=/data/openclaw/linkedin-tokens.json
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
PENDING_DIR="$BASE/pending"

# OAuth check
ACCESS_TOKEN=*** -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].access_token // empty' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)
DISPLAY_NAME=$(jq -r --arg u "$UID" '.[$u].display_name // "there"' "$TOKEN_STORE" 2>/dev/null)

# Check if brief already sent today
BRIEF_FLAG="$PENDING_DIR/.brief-sent-$TODAY"
[ -f "$BRIEF_FLAG" ] && echo "ALREADY_SENT=true" || echo "ALREADY_SENT=false"

# Load brand voice pillars and tone
PILLARS=""
TONE=""
if [ -f "$BASE/profile.md" ]; then
  PILLARS=$(grep -A 10 "Content Pi

ALREADY_SENT=false
UID=264468965
NAME=Andrii Bidochko
TODAY=2026-07-10
POSTS_TODAY=0
PILLARS_BLOCK=## Content Pillars

1. Business & entrepreneurship insights
2. Technology & AI innovations
3. Personal development & mindset

## Company / Product Context

- **Company:** UBOS (ubos.tech)
- **What it does:** AI Agent Orchestration Platform - build, host, and manage AI Agents in minutes using open-source tools (Langchain, CrewAI, Autogen, Vertex AI), 100+ LLM models, one-click cloud/on-premise deployment
- **Key differentiators:** No vendor lock-in (open-source, multi-cloud), unlimited agents as microservices, works with any LLM including self-hosted (Llama, Deepseek)
TONE_BLOCK=# LinkedIn Brand Voice - Andrii Bidochko

**Owner:** 264468965
**Last updated:** 2026-07-06

𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗔𝗜 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘄𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗱𝗼𝘄𝗻 𝗹𝗮𝘀𝘁 𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗸. 𝗬𝗼𝘂 𝗱𝗶𝗱𝗻'𝘁 𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗲.

Claude Fable 5 went down for weeks. Only 1 in 10 enterprises had auto-failure detection.

Here's the gap nobody's talking about.

Most teams built their AI stack for happy paths:
→ Model responds
→ Agent completes task
→ Everything works great

But what happens when the model goes down?

Silent failures. Stuck workflows. Customers waiting. No alerts.

The enterprises that survived the outage had TWO things:
1. Multi-model fallback (switch to GPT, Gemini, open-source)
2. Health checks that actually ping the model — not just the API

If your monitoring says "green" but your agent is frozen, you don't have observability. You have a dashboard.

The real test: Can you detect a failure BEFORE your customers tell you?

When did you last test your AI failure detection?

#AI #AIAgents #Reliability #EnterpriseAI #Monitoring

📝 Post 2/3 — Technology & AI innovations

𝗖𝗵𝗮𝘁𝗚𝗣𝗧 𝗷𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗿. 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁'𝘀 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗲𝘅𝗰𝘂𝘀𝗲?

Restaurants now take orders directly from ChatGPT and Claude. No app. No setup. Just AI-to-business transactions.

This is the new normal.

Your customers will expect the same. They'll ask their AI assistant to:
→ Book your service
→ Buy your product
→ Schedule a meeting
→ Get support

Can your business handle an AI customer?

Most companies are still optimizing for human clicks. Meanwhile, AI agents are becoming the new interface.

The question isn't "should we support AI transactions?" It's "how fast can we plug in?"

If a restaurant can do it with zero setup, what's stopping you?

#AI #Ecommerce #DigitalTransformation #FutureOfBusiness #AIAgents

Source: Restaurants accept AI orders (https://venturebeat.com/technology/restaurants-can-now-accept-orders-placed-directly-from-chatgpt-and-claude-thanks-to-squares-new-low-fee-no-setup-integration)

📝 Post 3/3 — Technology & AI innovations

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗔𝗜 𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴. 𝗛𝗲𝗿𝗲'𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝘆.

Everyone's chasing the most autonomous, most intelligent, most powerful AI agent.

But the teams shipping real value? They're doing the opposite.

They're building BORING agents:
→ One clear task
→ Defined success criteria
→ Human in the loop for edge cases
→ Fails gracefully, not spectacularly

Boring means predictable. Predictable means trustworthy. Trustworthy means adoption.

The flashiest AI demo you saw last week? Probably broken in production.

The boring agent that just works? That's the one getting renewed.

Stop optimizing for impressive. Start optimizing for reliable.

What's the most boring AI agent you could ship this week?

#AI #AIAgents #EnterpriseAI #SoftwareEngineering #Reliability
